failure_modes: Do not turn the move into a fixed journal paragraph template or repeat the theorem in looser words.
Do not use exposition to conceal an unresolved mechanism, assumption, or boundary.
moves.functional_name: Expose the fixed benchmark before the operative economic force
moves.intended_reader_update: The reader can distinguish the natural benchmark from the new force and use that distinction to predict the affected margin.
non_applicability: Do not apply before the benchmark and mechanism are current, accepted, and economically interpretable.
trigger_conditions: The reader encounters a result before being able to state what the natural benchmark holds fixed.
A formally correct retell does not support a prediction for a nearby environment.
valid_variants: A hand-solvable example may reveal the same benchmark-to-force contrast before the general statement.
A short benchmark paragraph may precede a theorem when the example would add notation rather than insight.

source.citation: Emir Kamenica and Matthew Gentzkow (2011), Bayesian Persuasion, American Economic Review 101(6):2590-2615, doi:10.1257/aer.101.6.2590
source.functional_summary: The derived card tracks how a precise persuasion question and benchmark make the later information-design result economically legible.
non_applicability: Do not use when the benchmark or operative economic force remains scientifically unresolved.
source.paper_specific_nontransferable: Do not transfer wording, cadence, notation, examples, applications, or any author-specific voice.
source.transferable_content: State the natural benchmark and operative choice before asking the reader to absorb the general characterization.

source.citation: Hal R. Varian (1997), How to Build an Economic Model in Your Spare Time, The American Economist 41(2):3-10
source.functional_summary: The derived card records the function of beginning from a concrete economic question and simple benchmark before generalization.
non_applicability: Do not use when the benchmark or operative economic force remains scientifically unresolved.
source.paper_specific_nontransferable: Do not transfer wording, cadence, notation, examples, applications, or any author-specific voice.
source.transferable_content: Use the simplest benchmark that exposes the economic delta, then let the general model answer the already-understood question.

source.citation: Econ Theorist AI v2 project-owned formal-first contrast fixture (2026)
source.functional_summary: The project-owned contrast is formally correct but withholds the benchmark and mechanism until after the proposition.
non_applicability: Do not use when the benchmark or operative economic force remains scientifically unresolved.
source.paper_specific_nontransferable: Do not transfer wording, cadence, notation, examples, applications, or any author-specific voice.
source.transferable_content: Use this card only to recognize the failure mode in which theorem comprehension does not yield a nearby economic prediction.

source.citation: Econ Theorist AI v2 project-owned empirical-template decoy (2026)
source.functional_summary: The decoy overlaps lexically with the theory problem but organizes evidence around identification and regression reporting.
non_applicability: Do not use when the benchmark or operative economic force remains scientifically unresolved.
source.paper_specific_nontransferable: Do not transfer wording, cadence, notation, examples, applications, or any author-specific voice.
source.transferable_content: No content is transferable into the theory core; the card exists to test explicit empirical exclusion.
